title: How to Keep Shipping When You Walk Away from Your Desk — Zack Proser, WorkOS
summary: Zack Proser argues that human attention, not agent speed, is the bottleneck in AI-assisted development. He presents a four-layer stack of signal agents, verification gates, self-improvement loops, and holistic monitoring to enable developers to ship code while away from their desks, framing this as a solution to AI-induced burnout.
writeup: Zack Proser of WorkOS contends that human attention, not agent speed, is the bottleneck in AI-assisted development, and he positions this constraint as a driver of AI-induced burnout. The talk lays out a four-layer stack: signal agents, verification gates, self-improvement loops, and holistic monitoring, designed to let developers keep shipping code while away from their desks. Supporting concepts include voice commands, logic gates, git worktree, MCP, and agentic workflows.
